config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu justru menjadi batas agar mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Dalam pengalaman saya, kalau “demi masyarakat” dipakai terlalu luas, sering kali yang lemah yang paling dirugikan dan kebebasan dasar jadi mudah dikorbankan. Yang lebih adil adalah mencari keseimbangan: kepentingan umum dijaga, tetapi hak individu tetap dihormati kecuali dalam keadaan yang benar-benar mendesak dan jelas alasannya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically come before the individual, because that can easily become an excuse for suppressing basic rights. From my perspective in China, people often value social stability and collective responsibility, and I understand why that matters, especially when disorder can hurt everyone. But if the rights of the individual are treated as secondary too often, the result can be abuse of power and ordinary people having too little protection. The better principle is balance: society can set limits when there is a clear public need, but individual rights should not be sacrificed just because the majority or authorities find it convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” memang sering terdengar kuat, tetapi dalam praktiknya bisa dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, apalagi kalau pengawasannya lemah. Hak individu itu penting justru karena menjadi pagar agar negara, mayoritas, atau kelompok kuat tidak seenaknya mengorbankan orang kecil. Yang paling masuk akal menurut saya adalah keseimbangan: kepentingan bersama boleh dibatasi hak individu hanya dalam kondisi benar-benar jelas, perlu, dan proporsional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should routinely take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize collective stability, public order, and shared responsibility, because those values can prevent chaos and help a large society function. But I have also seen how easily “for the good of society” can become a broad excuse for overreach, and once individual rights are treated as secondary, ordinary people often have too little protection. The right answer is not to choose one side absolutely, but to require clear necessity, proportionality, and real limits on power whenever individual rights are restricted.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi umum” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan keputusan yang merugikan orang kecil, apalagi kalau kontrol publik dan penegakan hukumnya lemah. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru hak individu diperlukan sebagai batas agar negara, mayoritas, atau kelompok kuat tidak semena-mena. Jadi, yang lebih tepat menurut saya adalah keseimbangan yang ketat: pembatasan hak hanya boleh dilakukan kalau benar-benar perlu, jelas alasannya, dan proporsional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize collective stability and public order, because a large society does need coordination and shared responsibility, but I have also seen how easily “for the common good” can be used to justify overreach. If individual rights are treated as secondary too often, ordinary people end up with too little protection against power, whether that power comes from the state, the majority, or local authorities. The fairest approach is balance: society may restrict rights only when there is a clear, necessary, and proportionate public reason, not simply because it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
